title: Welcome to PinPlay — Question type showcase

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — MCQ (single choice): Best for fast concept checks, vocab, dates and formulas. Write one clearly correct option and 3 plausible distractors so guessing is risky but the answer is unambiguous.

Q: Which planet in our solar system is the largest?
questions.audioText: Which planet in our solar system is the largest?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Multiple-select: Use when two or more answers are correct. Make the instruction explicit (e.g. "select all that apply") so students don't stop after the first match.

Q: Which of these are prime numbers? Select all that apply.
questions.audioText: Which of these are prime numbers? Select all that apply.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — True/False: Great for crisp factual claims. Avoid words like "sometimes" or "usually" — ambiguity turns T/F into a coin flip.

Q: True or False — Light travels faster than sound.
questions.audioText: True or False: Light travels faster than sound.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Typed answer (auto-graded): List every accepted spelling and synonym. Matching is case-insensitive and trims whitespace, but it's still literal — a missing accent or a typo will mark wrong unless you add it.

Q: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.audioText: What is the chemical symbol for gold?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Typed answer (teacher-graded): Leave the accepted list empty and the same question becomes a short-answer task you grade by hand after the quiz. Use this when you want one short sentence per student that you'll read.

Q: In one sentence, what makes a good study habit?
questions.audioText: In one sentence, what makes a good study habit?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Open response: Always teacher-graded. After the quiz you can award points, write corrections each student sees, and tag the best answers as "model answers" to project to the class.

Q: Describe one experiment you would run if you could spend a day on Mars.
questions.audioText: Describe one experiment you would run if you could spend a day on Mars.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Context gap (fill-in-the-blank): Use ____ in the prompt for each blank. The gaps list below maps in order — comma-separated values in one gap accept synonyms (e.g. "big, large").

Q: The Amazon rainforest lies mostly in the country of ____, and is often called the planet's ____.
questions.audioText: Fill in the blanks. The Amazon rainforest lies mostly in the country of blank, and is often called the planet's blank.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Match pairs: Perfect for vocab, capitals, formulas, dates — anything associative. Keep both columns parallel in style so the matching logic isn't given away by formatting.

Q: Match each country with its capital city.
questions.audioText: Match each country with its capital city.

questions.prompt: She don't likes pizza on Fridays.
questions.audioText: Find and fix the two mistakes in this sentence: She don't likes pizza on Fridays.
questions.corrected: She doesn't like pizza on Fridays.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Word puzzle: Students reorder shuffled tokens. Keep the item count between 4 and 9 so it stays readable on phones. Works for sentences, formulas, or timelines.

Q: Put the words in the right order to form a famous Neil Armstrong line.
questions.audioText: Put the words in the right order to form a famous Neil Armstrong line.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Slider estimate: Use for any "guess the number" — years, distances, percentages. The margin (none / low / medium / high / maximum) sets how close the answer must be to count.

Q: In what year did humans first land on the Moon?
questions.audioText: In what year did humans first land on the Moon?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Pin the image: Students tap a spot on your picture. Replace this placeholder with a real image (upload, paste from clipboard, or search the web). Use pinMode "all" to require every zone, or "any" if hitting one is enough.

Q: Pin the correct location on the image.
questions.audioText: Pin the correct location on the image.

questions.prompt: 💡 Tip — Audio question: A short clip plays before students answer. Use TTS (instant, pick voice and language) or upload your own recording. Tap the ▶ play button to hear the clip, then choose your answer below.
questions.audioText: Listen carefully. Tomorrow will be sunny in the morning, with a chance of light rain in the evening. Temperatures will reach twenty-two degrees Celsius.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Speaking: Records the student's voice for the time limit you set (15–30s is usually right). Best for small classes where you'll listen back. Works on most modern phones and laptops.

Q: In 20 seconds, introduce yourself and say one thing you enjoyed learning recently.
questions.audioText: In twenty seconds, introduce yourself and say one thing you enjoyed learning recently.

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Poll mode (isPoll: true): Turns any MCQ into an anonymous vote with no scoring and no correct answer. Use it for warm-ups, confidence checks, or quick opinion gathers at any point in the lesson.

Q: How are you feeling about today's lesson so far?
questions.audioText: How are you feeling about today's lesson so far?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Image on any question: Every type can have a picture. Upload from your computer, paste with Ctrl+V, or search the web from inside the builder.

Q: Looking at the screenshot of the builder above, which of these is NOT a way to add an image to your question?
questions.audioText: Looking at the screenshot of the builder, which of these is not a way to add an image to your question?

answers.text: 🤖 Generate one with AI from a prompt
🌐 Search the web from inside the builder

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Video clip: Paste a YouTube, Vimeo or direct video URL and set start/end seconds to trim to the exact moment. The clip plays inline with the question, before students answer.

Q: After watching the clip, where is the person filming?
questions.audioText: After watching the clip, where is the person filming?

questions.prompt:
💡 Tip — Timer (timeLimit in seconds): Set 0 for no timer (default — best for most classroom learning). Use a positive value to add retrieval pressure. This question has a 15-second timer to demo the feel.

Q: Quick! What is 7 × 8?
questions.audioText: Quick! What is seven times eight?
